info.title: flightsearchapi
info.description: Case Study - Flight Search Api (Java 21, Spring Boot, Mongodb, JUnit, Spring Security, JWT, Docker, Kubernetes, Prometheus, Grafana, Github Actions (CI/CD) )

tags.name: Search Flight
tags.description: Endpoints for searching flights based on given criteria.

externalDocs.description: Spring Boot Actuator Web API Documentation

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Handles user authentication and authorization operations.

tags.name: Flight Management
tags.description: Endpoints for managing flights including creation, retrieval, updating, and deletion.

tags.name: Airport Management
tags.description: Endpoints for managing airports including creation, retrieval, updating, and deletion.

get.description: Retrieves a flight by its ID. Accessible by both ADMIN and USER roles.

put.description: Updates an existing flight by its ID. Accessible by ADMIN only.

delete.description: Deletes a flight by its ID. Accessible by ADMIN only.

get.description: Retrieves an airport by its ID. Accessible by both ADMIN and USER roles.

put.description: Updates an existing airport by its ID. Accessible by ADMIN only.

delete.description: Deletes an airport by its ID. Accessible by ADMIN only.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of flights. Accessible by both ADMIN and USER roles.

post.description: Creates a new flight and saves it to the database. Accessible by ADMIN only.
Searches for flights based on departure, arrival airports, departure date, and optionally return date. Accessible by ADMIN and USER roles.
Registers a new user in the system.
Refreshes an expired access token using a valid refresh token.
Invalidates the provided token, effectively logging out the user.
Authenticates a user and returns an access and refresh token.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of airports. Accessible by both ADMIN and USER roles.

post.description: Creates a new airport and saves it to the database. Accessible by ADMIN only.
